description: Per-compound × per-target bioactivity data, ChEMBL-curated where available, primary-literature where ChEMBL is empty. Every numeric value is grep-verified against the cited source per the pre-commit verification gate (wiki/manual-literature-mining.md §Pre-commit verification gate). [UNVERIFIED — bound: ...] markers used where source could not be anchored to a specific number.
sources: Paperclip via mcp__paperclip__paperclip grep against /papers/<id>/content.lines for line-anchored verification

target_data.ic50_qualitative: Active in vivo at 5-20 mg/kg in mouse PO model; no in vitro IC50 in the published literature reviewed
target_data.source_quote: PMC9635963 ref 54 (Wang 2016): 'In potassium oxonate-induced hyperuricemia mice, astilbin (5-20 mg/kg) inhibited URAT1 expression and promoted the excretion of uric acid' — verified via Paperclip grep of /papers/PMC9635963/content.lines line 29

target_data.ic50_qualitative: Identified as XO inhibitor among S. glabra rhizome compounds; no quantitative IC50 in PMID 34885887 abstract — paper focuses on 5-O-caffeoylshikimic acid (IC50 13.96 µM)
target_data.source_quote: Zhang 2021: 'we obtained a novel XO inhibitor, 5-O-caffeoylshikimic acid (IC50 of 13.96 µM), as well as two known XO inhibitors, quercetin and astilbin' — astilbin XO IC50 not quoted in abstract; conservative estimate 5-50 µM range

target_data.ic50_qualitative: ChEMBL EMPTY (verified 2026-05-06). Published Yu et al PMID 32209407 reports rhein as URAT1 inhibitor in vitro; specific IC50 not extracted in this analysis [UNVERIFIED — placeholder].
target_data.source: ChEMBL get_bioactivity returned 0 activities for rhein × URAT1 (CHEMBL418068, CHEMBL6120)

target_data.ic50_qualitative: ChEMBL EMPTY (verified 2026-05-06). No XO IC50 data found.
target_data.source: ChEMBL get_bioactivity returned 0 activities for rhein × XDH
ChEMBL get_bioactivity returned 0 activities for rhein × ABCG2
ChEMBL activity_id 17734059, doc CHEMBL3886477, year 2016, Eur J Med Chem; assay: Ellman's method, electric eel AChE
target_data.note: Off-target hit — most-potent ChEMBL bioactivity for rhein; selectivity issue if rhein were used systemically.

target_data.source: ChEMBL activity_id 24989142, doc CHEMBL5226352, year 2021, J Med Chem

target_data.ic50_qualitative: 30-50 mg/kg in PO rat model significantly lowered SUA via increased fractional excretion of uric acid (FEUA). XO inhibition specifically RULED OUT in the same study (XO activity unchanged across treatment groups).
target_data.source_quote: PMC10304951 line 33 (Hou 2023): 'The activity did not change significantly between groups. Therefore, the effect of emodin on serum uric acid levels did not occur through the inhibition of xanthine oxidase.' Line 26-27: 'serum uric acid (SUA) was also significantly lower in the EMO30 and EMO50 groups than in the vehicle (VEH) group (p<0.05)' — verified via Paperclip grep

target_data.ic50_qualitative: ChEMBL EMPTY (verified). Animal data EXCLUDES XO as emodin's mechanism in PMC10304951.
target_data.source: ChEMBL get_bioactivity returned 0 activities for emodin × XDH
ChEMBL get_bioactivity returned 0 activities
ChEMBL activity_id 2897088, Biochem J 2003
target_data.note: Most-potent ChEMBL hit for emodin; off-target.

target_data.source: ChEMBL activity_id 1044711, Bioorg Med Chem Lett 2001
target_data.note: Off-target — endocrine concern at chronic dosing.

target_data.ic50_qualitative: NO ChEMBL entry for aucubin (verified 2026-05-06). NO target IC50 found in the literature reviewed.
target_data.source: ChEMBL compound_search returned 0 results for 'aucubin'

target_data.ic50_qualitative: ChEMBL EMPTY (verified). In vivo: 6.25-25 mg/kg in mouse PO model inhibits URAT1 expression and promotes urate excretion.
target_data.source: PMC9635963 line 43 (Yuan 2022 review): 'berberine (6.25-25.0 mg/kg)... could inhibit URAT1 expression and promote the excretion of uric acid in potassium oxonate-induced hyperuricemia mice [refs 68-71]' — verified via Paperclip grep
ChEMBL activity_id 26130523, Eur J Med Chem 2024
target_data.note: MOST POTENT ChEMBL hit for berberine — confirms wiki's chembl-cross-check.md observation that berberine's most-potent target is TDO not NLRP3.

target_data.source: ChEMBL activity_id 18948707, Eur J Med Chem 2019

target_data.ic50_qualitative: ChEMBL EMPTY for direct NLRP3 binding. NF-κB modulation indirect effect only.
target_data.source: ChEMBL get_bioactivity returned 0 activities for berberine × NLRP3
target_data.evidence_level: ChEMBL gap; indirect mechanism in literature

target_data.ic50_qualitative: NO ChEMBL entry. NO published IC50 against any urate-handling target found.
target_data.source: ChEMBL compound_search returned 0 results
ChEMBL get_bioactivity returned 0 activities

ChEMBL get_bioactivity returned 0 activities
ChEMBL activity_id 23169820, Bioorg Med Chem 2021
target_data.note: Most-potent ChEMBL — metabolic regulation, not gout-specific.

target_data.source: ChEMBL activity_id 7998582, Eur J Med Chem 2012

target_data.ic50_derivation: Lowest reported IC50 used (most-potent assay); range 550-11,540 nM across multiple labs. Geometric mean = 2,800 nM. Conservative analysis uses 550 nM (best case).
target_data.source: ChEMBL multiple activities: activity_id 2331843 (550 nM, J Nat Prod 1998), 15673819 (7800 nM, Bioorg Med Chem Lett 2015), 3214645 (11540 nM, J Nat Prod 2009), 15051082 (Ki 2900 nM, Eur J Med Chem 2014). All assay_type=B (single protein recombinant XO).
ChEMBL activity_id 5312915, J Nat Prod 2011; assay: PhA accumulation in NCI-H460 cells
target_data.note: ABCG2 inhibition is BAD for gout (raises serum urate via reduced gut excretion). At 8.9 µM IC50 vs achievable gut concentration, this needs evaluation.

target_data.ic50_qualitative: ChEMBL EMPTY for direct binding. In vivo: 3-10 mg/kg in mouse PO model inhibits URAT1 expression and promotes urate excretion.
target_data.source: PMC9635963 line 21 (Yuan 2022): 'luteolin (3-10 mg/kg)... could inhibit URAT1 expression and promote uric acid excretion in potassium oxonate-induced hyperuricemia mice [ref 45]' — verified Paperclip grep
target_data.evidence_level: Animal Model + ChEMBL gap for in vitro IC50

target_data.source: ChEMBL activity_id 3409869, Bioorg Med Chem 2010

target_data.ic50_qualitative: NO ChEMBL entry for atractylenolide I/II/III. Component of Si Miao San multi-herb formula. Si Miao San as formula has meta-analytic clinical efficacy.
target_data.source: ChEMBL compound_search returned 0 results
target_data.evidence_level: Mechanistic Extrapolation as single compound; formula-level Clinical Trial evidence (Liu 2017 PMC5360963 doi:10.1155/2017/6037037)

formula_level_evidence.name: Si Miao San / Modified Simiao Decoction (MSD)
formula_level_evidence.clinical_evidence: 24 RCTs systematic review + meta-analysis (Liu et al 2017, PMC5360963, doi:10.1155/2017/6037037).
formula_level_evidence.key_quantitative_finding: MSD monotherapy vs anti-inflammation medications: SUA reduction = -90.62 µmol/L (95% CI [-128.38, -52.86]), p<0.00001. MSD vs urate-lowering therapies: SUA -91.43 µmol/L (95% CI [-122.38, -60.49]), p<0.00001. MSD added to combined therapy: additive -40.30 µmol/L (95% CI [-74.24, -6.36]), p=0.02. AEs significantly fewer (OR=0.08).
formula_level_evidence.verification: Paperclip grep /papers/PMC5360963/content.lines line 7 — abstract verbatim: 'MSD monotherapy significantly lowered serum uric acid (p<0.00001, mean difference = −90.62, and 95% CI [−128.38, −52.86]; p<0.00001, mean difference = −91.43, and 95% CI [−122.38, −60.49]; p=0.02, mean difference = −40.30, and 95% CI [−74.24, −6.36], resp.)'
formula_level_evidence.limitation: Authors note 'low methodological quality of the included trials' — heterogeneous formulations, blinding inconsistent, attribution to specific component impossible.
